text:
There once was a cheerful cat named Churchill. He lived in a cozy little cottage near a lush wood.

Every morning Churchill would meow playfully as he stretched upon his window seat. The sun would sometimes peek through the fluffy clouds, casting dainty diamonds onto his fur.

One day Churchill decided he wanted to visit a shiny new fountain in town. He meowed loudly – birds flew forth!

After weaving through a field of wildflowers, Churchill arrived at the fountain. He tip-toed gleefully and drank deeply. Then he meowed happily to an invisible friend.

His adventure made Churchill very proud indeed. Just then, a strange wind rustled through the trees.

Intruders! Churchill cried. Off he went.

Into the wood he dashed, graceful as a squirrel. The stars twinkled above as he ran. Soon he found a secret glade.

There stood Cupid. Cupid had love poems written all over him.

“I’ve never loved anyone…I think,” Cupid sighed
